summary_text: **Current state (confirmed)** - `launchd` job is registered and points to the right runtime: [com.cursorvers.x-auto-scheduler.plist](/Users/masayuki/Library/LaunchAgents/com.cursorvers.x-auto-scheduler.plist) - But `launchd` is not the owner (`active count = 0`, `last exit code = 1`) and is repeatedly failing. - Live scheduler is currently manual, under tmux/Claude (`PID 60694`, `PPID 2923`), not launchd. - Collision spam is continuous in logs: [scheduler.err.log](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/logs/scheduler.err.log) and [scheduler.log](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/logs/scheduler.log) (`Another scheduler is running` every ~30s). **Safest cutover sequence (no duplicate-post risk)** 1. Choose a no-due window (at least 5 minutes before next scheduled post). 2. Capture baseline. ```bash launchctl print gui/$(id -u)/com.cursorvers.x-auto-scheduler ps -axo pid=,ppid=,etime=,command= | rg '/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py' tail -n 40 /Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/logs/scheduler.log tail -n 40 /Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/logs/scheduler.err.log cat /Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/logs/heartbeat.json ``` 3. Stop launchd job first to remove restart race during handover. ```bash launchctl bootout gui/$(id -u) /Users/masayuki/Library/LaunchAgents/com.cursorvers.x-auto-scheduler.plist ``` 4. Gracefully stop the manual scheduler owner (`60694` now). ```bash kill -TERM 60694 sleep 3 ps -p 60694 -o pid= ``` 5. Ensure there is no scheduler process left. ```bash ps -axo pid=,ppid=,command= | rg '/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py' || true ``` 6. Start launchd ownership explicitly. ```bash launchctl bootstrap gui/$(id -u) /Users/masayuki/Library/LaunchAgents/com.cursorvers.x-auto-scheduler.plist launchctl kickstart -k gui/$(id -u)/com.cursorvers.x-auto-scheduler ``` 7. Verify single-owner runtime. ```bash launchctl print gui/$(id -u)/com.cursorvers.x-auto-scheduler ps -axo pid=,ppid=,etime=,command= | rg '/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py' cat /Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/logs/heartbeat.json tail -n 60 /Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/logs/scheduler.err.log ``` 8. Observe for 2-3 polling cycles (~3 minutes). Success criteria: - one scheduler process only - no new `Another scheduler is running` - heartbeat timestamp keeps advancing **Rollback (if launchd does not stabilize within 3 minutes)** 1. Stop launchd job: ```bash launchctl bootout gui/$(id -u) /Users/masayuki/Library/LaunchAgents/com.cursorvers.x-auto-scheduler.plist ``` 2. Restart manual scheduler in controlled shell/tmux: ```bash cd /Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto /Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/venv/bin/python /Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py ``` 3. Re-check heartbeat/logs. **Key risks to watch** - Cutover too close to a due slot can delay posting by one poll interval. - Skipping step 3 (bootout first) allows launchd race while manual process is still alive. - Leaving Claude/tmux automation unchanged may reintroduce manual ownership later. - False “healthy” if you check only process existence and not `heartbeat + collision lines` together. If you want, I can execute this cutover sequence live in a controlled window and report each checkpoint.
